And Bob, for those of you who aren't on this, oh my God, you don't know what you're missing. Shame on you. Anyway, your tritonal closing pattern. Let me just tell you how many times I have coached someone who was in the looping process and they couldn't quite get the customer to say yes and simply by taking them through the final loop into this soft tritonal close where you do the up tone, sound fair enough? I've closed a thousand sales just to coaching other people like that. This is so powerful. So you give me one shot and believe me, the only problem you'll have is you didn't buy more. Sound fair, three tonalities stacked together. It's just there's something about it that just makes it work. Logically, right? Well, yeah, certainty. And it's almost like it's like a bit of jiu-jitsu going on. You know, it's like you're certainty and then they feel you just pressure and you'll lay off and they collapse into the lack of energy. Like, you give me one shot and believe me, you'll be glad. It's like that low energy, they collapse into your energy. Be soft. You cannot close someone with high pressure. It doesn't work. The way we close with a straight line is through logic and reason through emotional future pacing. We line up the core elements of the system. We picked the lock to their buying strategy. It's not about exerting high pressure. It's not that at all. Don't do that. You be soft, be mellow, be persuasive, be as smooth as silk as the sandals. That's how I want you to sound. So what do you do? Well, in this lesson here, the first lesson, it's just really about understanding about what these tonalities sound like. You got to practice them. Make sure you have a desk pawn. Make sure you have someone. If you're not working at a company, if you're not part of the corporate trade, just do it individually, find someone on the Wolf network that will work with you. Practice these tonalities. Get them to become unconsciously competent. I promise you do that. You're going to be in an amazing spot. Watch how bad. Watch how improper the opposite sounds. Jim, you give me one shot and believe me, you'll be really, really impressed. Sound good? Would you buy from someone who sounded that way? Just remember, something that almost helps to find out what the wrong way sounds like. People don't want to buy from the guy on their barking infomercial. You know why people bark on infomercials? Because it's the only way to get your attention when you're out there, you're vacuuming the floor, mopping the fucking ceiling, you're running a bath in a yellow screen at you, right? That doesn't go in anything but an infomercial. And even that's kind of old and bland. Remember that guy Tony on the little, the gazelle guy, right? He yelled at you. We all bought his shit because it was just so funny, right? Come on. That's not how you want to sound. Professionals don't talk. Professionals just say, listen, very simple. You give me a shot.
